replies.author: @JemieBridges
replies.text:
​@odojanghi!  The guy who suggested mexican namor.  Atlantis never ever existed. It was always a make believe hypothetical place.  A metaphor in a play.  By a famous person youve studied.  

But.  A sunken city did happen in recent history of the person who wrote the play.   Probably multiple times.   Tangent:  This also reoccurred on Japan, except it's a tsunami that sucks a city completely out to sea.  In fact this cycle was only averted in 2011 by Japanese Mayor Kotoku Wamura who spent all the towns goodwill on a multi-billion dollar wall.   He got made fun of until the tsunami actually came based upon some math he did previous natural disasters.  

Anyways this sorta thing happens around the world.  Eventually 7 sites for what COULD have been Atlantis were found.  (That is if Atlantis was ever real and not just a translation error)  On such site is super easy to access and get filming rights for is the location of South America.  It's an entire Inca level city that's visible from the ocean surface.   It's not ruins.  Just deeply underwater.  25 feet.   That's is the cheapest and easiest to reach, the water is warm so no need to worry about actors health.   Kinda vacation for the film crew.   The others are in cold harsh waters   even the Mediterranean city nearest Greece is uncomfortable to work in and is in bad shape due to you know 2-4 millenia passing lolz.

So we went with South American Namor.  Do you think Greek would have improved it?  That of course is how I thought of it all my life but after diving the sites and you know actually learning the language to read the play and searching for actual Atlantis, this is the advice I gave.  Surprise they took it, but it's the most accurate.  

Tiahuanaco off of Bolivia is what the Internet is showing me.  IDK if that's where I dived, I'd have to look in my attic at photos.

replies.author: @vicmrcd-n3l
replies.text:
​@joeberger3441 thats a bit of an oversimplification. Its actually pretty interesting and complex. Now if the arguement is something like "will this affect people in hospitals?" Then the answer is no... of course not lol

But why have things gotten to this point? 
And where do these reactions or takes come from?
What sparks things like this?
What is the metric in which we measure the "value" of something or by what systems can we look at something in a critical way? 

Just from an academic standpoint, this is actually a pretty complex issue. So its not just a simple "its because of this" type of thing. Its mulitple things working together in order to create "discourse" for lack of a better word.

replies.author: @joeberger3441
replies.text: ​​@vicmrcd-n3lI don't disagree that there are complexities behind the current trends.  Humans are by nature complex, social creatures. I'm simplifying it to cynicism because it is by definition a cynical take to suggest that a movie you haven't even seen yet is automatically unwatchable even when a critic known for being critical, gives it praise where it's due.  As for the complexities, I think a lot of it can be summed up with social media. Add social media been around in previous decades there would've been a lot more scrutiny with certain films. Political correctness was nothing new. Even if not as aggressive as wokeness (I'm not a fan of either just saying they have been around before).

replies.text:
​​@joeberger3441 well thats all true. There are elements to that. But i would also add that i wouldnt really aim it at "trends" but instead it is based on past performances.

Super short story. I remember my neighbor would tell me about this resturant he really liked and it was also slightly expensive. But it wasnt exactly "fancy" or anything.

So i asked him "why?" He explained that not only was the food really good but it was also consistent. So no matter who was on staff that day or what day of the week it was, he knew that if he went to order a steak that it would be cooked the same way everytime.

So because the service was good and consistent he knew he could order anything and have the same level of quality. So he recommended it to me.

So in terms of movies and hollywood, i understand not everything is going to be a masterpiece, thats understandable. 
But i think an inportant component to all of this is when someone gives a critque and everyone, including the actors or director or whoever starts insulting their audience and do a bunch of other silly stuff only reinforces these points.

So if my neighbor had said spmerhing like "my steak is undercooked" and then the waiter starts insulting him and then manager, then the cook and even the busboy gets involved. Hell even the other diners in the resturant.
He most likely wouldnt have gone back there and i would understand why.
Also they did make a great steak. So he lnew what he was talking about lol

Also i dont think that criticalDrinker has any blame on this because normally he is pretty consistent, so people tend to believe him accordingly.
So at this point if he changes his mind, he can do that.
At least he isnt afriad "hey i was wrong, its pretty alright".

Where as other people refuse to do it out of pride, spite or arrogence. Thats not how someone gets better in anything. You cant put your fingers in your ears and then cry ignorance or get mad when people bring up certain topics.
How can the films, or movies, or shows, or games, or whatever else get better if people dont want to listen to anyone else but their own Acolytes and their iwn reflection.

Its genuinely interesting what is happening, but if someone genuinely doesnt care then there no point in even discussing it. Its basicly worthless.

replies.author: @datacipher
replies.text:
My warning for people who haven’t watched it.  Thank me in 6 months lol. 

People saying it’s “good but not great”…. I’ll be charitable and say they have rose-color glasses.  This is what I wrote before: 

“This was BAD.  If this is better than Superman… I’m speechless.  The tone was all over the place and seldom good. Johnny looks like a weirdo… they barely use their powers. It’s not OVERTLY woke - but it is… only Sue can put up a fight against woman surfer… only Sue can beat Galactus - not by working together… just Sue.   They also have immigrant refugees, and climate change angles but don’t point it out - but it’s obvious.


Johnny’s angle with the surfer goes nowhere.  Ben - who does virtually nothing in this move has a boring subplot - where he also does virtually nothing.  Reed is reduced to a bumbling, stress-basket nerd.  Sue is their leader.  Her character was all over the place.  It’s tone - the aesthetic - coudl have been cool but was so cartoony and cheesy to the point where it was parody at times.  Many of us laughed out loud at moments - not in a good way.  In a way: I can’t believe this isn’t a skit way.  It started for me when they just had the ludicrous scene of juxtaposing the team flying their ship apart around a black hole with Sue on a table giving birth. Ok honey, make us invisible after your next contraction - seriously, and they weren’t trying to make it a joke. 


Galactus does nothing but walk around a bit - he sort of looked cool in the dark at first in the dark of his ship.  Then at moments he looks cool in the city like in the trailer… but like the trailer he looks better when you only see bits of him.  He looks a bit cheesy when you see his face which isn’t an awe inspiring, cosmic, higher level being… but looking a bit like… Michael rappapport in a bad Halloween outfit having a bad day.  Wish they had a better face on him.  

The biggest thing I can say is, don’t waste your money on this.  I PROMISE YOU , when you see it for free, you won’t be sad you didn’t see it till then, and I promise, you’ll have zero urge to ever watch it again.  I actually think the other 2 movies were more appealing than this.”

replies.author: @darklord884
replies.text:
​@RealFreedomMGI meant that the hypersensitivity itself is somewhat justifiable. The hypersensitivity was caused by the over the top progressive mentality perpetuated and ruining several larger media franchises over the years, so people became hypersensitive to it. It is still wrong to be hypersensitive, but understandable/justifiable considering the amount of over the top crappy woke stuff being shoved out nowadays. Not that being woke was those films/games' only problems, but wokeness became a sort of symbol of shoddy craftsmanship and there became this overeager association between the two, which even today is unfortunately often correct. 

Just look at Snow White, the main actresses of which have talked at length about how progressive and new and stunning and brave the movie is. And it was crap. That is not to say the movie's biggest problem is that it is woke, but the fact it was woke made many people think it would be bad, which ended up being correct, since it was. And this happened enough times that now any idea of progressive or woke thought can make people think a product will be bad. And this is not the fault of those hypersensitive to it, but those who made an abundance of bad progressive product in the first place.

text:
I feel like Drinker went into this movie with such rock bottom expectations that when it modestly exceeded them, he acted like a thirsty man in the desert being offered a glass of cloudy room temperature tap water.

replies.text: I really likes this version of F4, I think is the best these has been so far. Which doesnt set the bar high, imo it was quite on par with Superman

replies.author: @EliFennell
replies.text:
​@puupääSuperman had character development. Clark learns that his real parents, the ones he got his values from, are the Kents, not his Kryptonian parents. The Justice Gang also learns that they should follow more in Clark's footsteps and take a more proactive role in defending the innocent. Clark also faces adversity and overcomes it.

You may not like those character arcs, but they have them. Who has a character arc in F4? Silver Surfette, sort of? None of the main cast. They don't change a bit. They don't learn anything. They face no real adversity at all. It's... fine, but totally generic, the definition of "and then" storytelling. Not saying Superman was gold, but it had something.

replies.author: @puupää
replies.text:
​@EliFennell To be honest, I dont rate movies by how steep a character arc was or wasn't. I am not a movie critic. To me it pretty much comes down to if something was entertaining or not. 
I loved F4 cartoons as a kid and I enjoyed how the characters were portrayed in this movie. I am not saying that it was a perfect movie, not by a long shot. But it was better than all the previous F4 movies.

replies.author: @EliFennell
replies.text: ​@puupääThis genre only dominated as long as it had compelling characters with arcs. When it stopped caring about that, these movies became "theme park rides", and you can only ride the same ride a few times before it gets dull no matter how much fun it might have been the first time or a few. So you might not care, but most people do. I'm not saying every movie needs to be Oscar worthy, but they can't just be people in tights and big special effects scenes.

Jeremiah 17:5
“This is what the Lord says: ‘Cursed is the one who trusts in man, who draws strength from mere flesh and whose heart turns away from the Lord.'” 

Deuteronomy 28:15
“However, if you do not obey the Lord your God and do not carefully follow all his commands and decrees I am giving you today, all these curses will come on you and overtake you.” 

Galatians 3:10
“For all who rely on the works of the law are under a curse, as it is written: ‘Cursed is everyone who does not continue to do everything written in the Book of the Law.'” '

As we can see, we are under a curse in this generation.  We have turned away from the Lord to trust in men (like Trump) to save society.  Others have turned to Marxist ideologies (woke. "progressive" Liberalism) made up by atheistic men to try saving society.  
Furthermore, we see that we cannot earn salvation or be relieved of this curse by following religious laws (which unfortunately Trump will try doing in the near future, SEE: Noah/hide Laws), so what are the commands and decrees we are to follow if we want to get out from under God's curse?

Deuteronomy 18:15-19
15 “The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among you, from your brothers—it is to him you shall listen— 16 just as you desired of the Lord your God at Horeb on the day of the assembly, when you said, ‘Let me not hear again the voice of the Lord my God or see this great fire any more, lest I die.’ 17 And the Lord said to me, ‘They are right in what they have spoken. 18 I will raise up for them a prophet like you from among their brothers. *And I will put my words in his mouth, and he shall speak to them all that I command him. 19 And whoever will not listen to my words that he shall speak in my name, I myself will require it of him.*

John 6:27-29
27 Do not labor for the food which perishes, but for the food which endures to everlasting life, which the Son of Man will give you, because God the Father has set His seal on Him.”

28 Then they said to Him, “What shall we do, that we may work the works of God?”

29 Jesus answered and said to them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in Him whom He sent.”

Matthew 22:37-40
37 Jesus said unto him, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind.

38 This is the first and great commandment.

39 And the second is like unto it, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

40 On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets.

If you do not want to be under a curse, put your faith in Christ, love Him and love others.  I know this is too much to ask of most of you since you would rather live without Christ and hate your enemies while feeling self-righteous about yourselves. 


Galatians 3:13-18
13 *Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law,* being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree:

14 That the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.

15 Brethren, I speak after the manner of men; Though it be but a man's covenant, yet if it be confirmed, no man disannulleth, or addeth thereto.

16 Now to Abraham and his seed were the promises made. He saith not, And to seeds, as of many; but as of one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.

17 And this I say, that the covenant, that was confirmed before of God in Christ, the law, which was four hundred and thirty years after, cannot disannul, that it should make the promise of none effect.

18 For if the inheritance be of the law, it is no more of promise: but God gave it to Abraham by promise.

Romans 3:21-31
God’s Righteousness Through Faith
21 *But now the righteousness of God apart from the law is revealed, being witnessed by the Law and the Prophets, 22 even the righteousness of God, through faith in Jesus Christ, to all and on all who believe. For there is no difference; 23 for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,* 24 being justified freely by His grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus, 25 whom God set forth as a propitiation by His blood, through faith, to demonstrate His righteousness, because in His forbearance God had passed over the sins that were previously committed, 26 to demonstrate at the present time His righteousness, that He might be just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus.

Boasting Excluded
27 Where is boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? Of works? No, but by the law of faith. 28 *Therefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith apart from the deeds of the law. 29 Or is He the God of the Jews only? Is He not also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, of the Gentiles also, 30 since there is one God who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith.* 31 Do we then make void the law through faith? Certainly not! On the contrary, we establish the law.

replies.author: @axelhopfinger533

@Stinkyremy Agreed, it's a quite flawed interpretation of the books. Especially the presence of Zendaya alone almost ruins it all for me. 
But it's also still grand cinema on its own merits. A proper spectacle, especially impressive when watched in a modern theater with a big Dolby Atmos sound system. A cinematic achievement, especially in the sound design department.
And the CGI is near flawless.
So despite its flaws in adaption of the source material, still well worth experiencing.

All the dudes come off as hypereffeminate (par for the course these days but doesn't mean i'm gonna watch the sh*t cuz all the men in Hollywood have no balls, that's their problem not mine) and both of the primary featured women are unattractive with bland characterization as well.

Not a hard pass, just an easy one. I'll watch it on the high seas until Disney/Lucasfilm/Marvel/Pixar get their sh*t together. replies.author: @Frosty_tha_Snowman
replies.text:
​​@jakecahill7795I don't really have 1 favorite movie... there are a lot of genres to match different moods and feelings, so choosing 1 that is without a doubt, "my favorite" feels disingenuous. I also prefer a good TV show to a good movie - but to kind of answer your question: I like the Lord of the Rings trilogy a lot, especially the Two Towers, with the battle of Helms Deep being one of the most well-made pieces of cinema ever (aside from Legolas surfing down the stairs on a shield, which felt like a feverish delusion) - the movie Parasite was an incredible story and was phenomenally well made. The Hateful 8 was a lot of fun and very interesting to watch play out. The first Spiderman is a classic. Logan is everything a Superhero movie should be, and the Joker was just unbelievable and unexpected, but completely blew me away. As far as "dumb fun" goes, the first Ironman was good, along with Avengers Infinity War and Endgame. The 1st and 2nd Jurassic Park were also in that same category, with the 3rd also being dumb fun, with it leaning a bit more toward dumb than fun... 

As for TV shows, it doesn't really get much better than Peaky Blinders, Hell on Wheels, and the Punisher. Squid Game is also a major standout - and I feel like I have to mention Breaking Bad. The Penguin was also just... in a category of its own - feeling very similar to the Joker. The first few seasons of the Walking Dead were also top tier, but a lot of people seem to have forgotten that because the show self destructed so spectacularly, and the series name became a metaphor for its existence. 

Not sure I gave you what you wanted, but there it is. 😂

She also needed assistance to do it - the other F4 were knocking down buildings so he can't fight back against the push, disabling and distracting him, and flying ahead to activate the portal.

Edit::
I just realized what happened. You watched Penguin0's review of it without actually watching the movie and are just parroting what he said. He made it sound like Sue 1v1's Galactus in his video. His review was missing a lot of details, but I think that was to preserve our enjoyment of it without spoiling 100% of the movie. It's a decent movie in my opinion. Go see it yourself and form your own thoughts rather than taking someone else's.

replies.text: Sue Storm in the comics has killed a Celestial solo, and sustained a force field in space for WEEKS so large it prevented ANY sunlight from reaching the Earth while Reed worked on defeating an existential threat to the planet. She's also covered the ENTIRE planet in a force field for several hours. A force field big enough and strong enough to knock over a 700 foot giant is not beyond her range. Add to that Galactus' ignorance of their abilities, and his trying to perform a very delicate task at the time they attacked him, and his choosing not to just start blasting the place and risk possibly destroying his prize, and it is pretty believable they could, with the surfers help, momentarily trip him up. Physics still apply.

replies.author: @DReppin2009
replies.text:
If you've been around long enough to see Iron Man all the way up to the new F4, this doesn't surprise you. DC properties are always met with a certain level of over-criticism and analyzing. They don't get to just be passable like Marvel anymore. I actually liken the MCU to anime greats like Naruto and One Piece, where filler episodes get a pass because the rest of the product is good. MCU goodwill is no joke.

DC properties since Snyder's Man of Steel have always been met with a lot more skepticism. There is an inherent bias for Marvel properties, even if some folks don't like to admit it, but that is okay.

It's on DC and Warner to EARN the goodwill of getting a pass for average to above average entertainment. Until then, the MCU will remain a level above, even at their lowest point.

As someone who feel like the new F4 is a bit overrated and Superman is a bit over criticized (personally they're both good for different reasons), I get annoyed seeing guys like Drinker choosing to turn a blind eye to some glaring issues in F4, all because it's better than the last slew of films in the universe.

replies.author: @Breno1997
replies.text:
For entertainment as a whole, I would say.

I found Expedition 33 to be fun and enjoyable. In short, a good game, but not amazing nor groundbreaking as people have been claiming it to be by any stretch of the imagination. 

That is how things are as of now. It is almost an adaptation measure — if we won't lower our expectations, we won't be able to enjoy most of the newest releases. 

Blokes around there that have more rigid criteria might be suffering through this hellish show. I know that I am. I am not paying to watch either Superman and nor Fantastic 4. Hell, I watched Ballerina in the theaters and it wasn't worth the money I spent on it, even in spite of the fun I had with it. I could have just downloaded it and watched it while eating a fine pizza back in my room.

replies.author: @eddielong8663
replies.text:
What utter rubbish. 'Mad Max - Fury Road' came out 10 years ago and was considered a masterpiece back then. While it obviously didn't start off the girlboss trope, it certainly helped to exasibate it. There was a small group of individuals who were critical of the pro-feminist angle of the film and how a franchise that was supposed to be about a male action hero was essentially hijacked by a strong independent female. And they were mocked and dismissed by just about everyone, including conservatively-minded men. Nowadays, that small group of people have much more company. 

It wouldn't be as well received by general audiences today as it was back then. The box office results of the more recent prequel demonstrate that perfectly. 

And don't even get me started on how nobody at the time seemed to care about all the wokeness of 'The Force Awakens'. 

We are much more sceptical now. Not less.

1 The whole Israelite community set out from Elim and came to the Desert of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after they had come out of Egypt. 2 In the desert the whole community grumbled against Moses and Aaron. 3 The Israelites said to them, “If only we had died by the Lord’s hand in Egypt! There we sat around pots of meat and ate all the food we wanted, but you have brought us out into this desert to starve this entire assembly to death.”

replies.author: @gildatheplant
replies.text: I can't speak for the last FF movie because I didn't see it, but the ones from the early 2000s main issue was casting. As individuals, Chris Evans and Michael Chiklis were perfectly cast, but Jessica Alba was way too young for Sue Storm and Ioan Gruffud didn't have enough gravitas for Mr. Fantastic. Sue is supposed to be almost a mother figure to Johnny, but Alba and Evans are the same age and Alba actually looked younger, so their dynamic didn't work. Without those core family ties a Fantastic Four movie can't work.

replies.author: @ModelLights
replies.text:
@4lackofbetter ' and want to have and protect their child'
It makes for a poorer movie though.  What's happening now?  Baby!  OK, what's happening now 15 minutes later?  BABY AGAIN!  And later?  MORE BABY!  You could hear Will Ferrell and Christopher Walken both in the background saying 'MORE BABY!'.
And realize, this is current Disney doing this.  'Let's make this movie all about the baby so no one can criticize it!'
Realize more, you could easily add far more plot, still have the main thing and motivation be the baby, and make a much better movie without much effort.
This is Transformers, using 'BABY!' to fill the plot holes and gaps and missing plot instead of 'ACTION SCENE' like they did for that movie.
Don't get me wrong I'd give it a 6, it wasn't terrible, but it was a bit thin.  Mostly it's just 'retro AI futurism' and 'baby'.
Galactus himself seemed fairly well done, Sue Storm effects were pretty good, etc.  It definitely wasn't terrible and better than the last few Marvel movies, but it is also easy to see ways it could have been better.

author: @missydakango6374
text:
Didn't hate it, but certainly there were a lot of uhhhhhwhat? moments I'm surprised didn't bother you.
1) Cool shot of Silver Surfer first arriving with like thousands of asteroids flying down toward Earth that...disappear? Did they land? Not as far as I know...
2) They go invisible to enter Galactus' ship but the Silver Surfer has no problem finding them and entering the ship. But later when they escape, they use invisibility to get away from her? Huh?
3) Why did Galactus get out of his ship to come to Earth? Didn't do it for the other planets. Was never shown how he knew Franklin would be in a crib in that plaza--sort of expected Reed to send him a message to offer it or something.
There were a couple of other little moments that I didn't buy, but those stuck out. (Oh, and yeah, what's with the ending of Thunderbolts where the FF ship is coming to that universe? That predates this movie, but the FF don't leave their universe at the end of this...?)

author: @carlrood4457
text:
It's ironic what you said because in the comics, The Thing is probably the most popular member of the GF. He was the star of Marvel 2 in 1 for a decade and has has a couple solo series of his own.
He used to host a floating superhero poker game. He had a history as heroic combat pilot long before getting his powers. He gets depressed over his appearance, but actually likes being a superhero.

He was also the only guy to look at Cap and Ironman in the original Civil War and nope out of the situation because it got in the way of helping people.

Heck, I'd argue Cap's "I can do tho all day" comes from Ben's fight with The Champion, an Elder of the Universe.

replies.author: @smartmouf3116
replies.text: Sheesh man. This movie just didn't do it for me. I was bored. It lacked action. There was no combat in the film whatsoever.  Just a whole lot of lounging, and talking. If you're gonna have Reed solve math problems for 90% of the screen time, at least have Grimm and Johnny off to the side fighting some goons or something. Sue was way too overpowered to the point where she made Galactus look weak. She basically handled him all by herself. Reed only used his powers to push buttons and pull levers. Grimm on the other hand just felt useless. He was there just to be there. His role just felt like that of an old retired grandpa who just didn't have the will to fight anymore. I give the movie a 6/10 at best. The plot could have been much better.

We can’t just discard all examples of a character doing some outrageous feat as “insert modern meme word”.

That’s too easy, and a bit lazy.  Obviously yes, she’s depicted as being ferociously powerful with what she can do when motivated.  As others have said, seems to be not totally out of line with comics. We’re just dealing with a more ‘normal’ Big Bad Guy in this Galactus.

If it was vs any other Marvel group, some way would have been found to have an Earth-level hero do something outrageous to accomplish the same thing. Plot armour is baked in to Super Heroes whether we like it or not.

Notable, but not offensively so.

author: @pam007
text:
What do you mean there’s no “girl boss”? Invisible Woman runs the entire show. She’s the face of the Fantastic Four in this version. There’s even that scene where she comes out like she’s at the UN, giving a speech. how sbout speach about she won’t let her baby be sacrificed. I get it  it was emotional but everyone just goes along with her plan, even though there’s no guarantee it will work. No one questions it. They just blindly agree instead of even considering giving the baby away to save the world.

And during the final fight — what did anyone actually do against Galactus? I understand he’s insanely powerful, but the others literally did nothing except Invisible Woman and maybe The Thing at the very end. Mr. Fantastic tries to poke Galactus with a needle and almost gets torn apart. Human Torch throws a little fire and that’s it.

Yes, Invisible Woman is clearly OP in this version, but she basically takes over the whole film while the rest of the team just fades into the background.

Also, what was the point of gender-swapping Silver Surfer? It had zero impact on the story. Human Torch tries to flirt with her briefly, but that goes nowhere — no deeper story, no meaningful development. Just surface-level changes for no reason

replies.author: @LeftHandEats
replies.text:
Hey, to be clear, Sue Storm is by far the most OP one out of them all in the comics. Molecule Man can’t even affect her shields, they can stop even Magic from gods to the point she uses them to seal spells and block telepathy. Plus the stuff she can create them is vastly beyond what they’ve ever shown in the movies. Recently in Ryan Norths run she put a force field over the entire sun to block it out! Johnny Storm helped her get close by absorbing all the heat from the sun which is also OP, and her fields can get so precise she can make tiny bubbles in someone’s brain blocking even synapses from allowing people to think. And she quite literally was able to hold off against MAD CELESTIALS with her force fields. They didn’t last forever, of course, but she held off against their attacks for a considerable period of time before they got through. Very few people can take on invisible woman in the comics, unless you’re above namor and she hulk, you’re toast. Cause she’s one shot namor and beat tf out of she hulk (though her regen/recovery prevents the knock outs from being ultimately effective. She’s also blocked attacks from gladiator and impossible man. She uses her shields as offensive weapons very well in the comics, basically like green lantern constructs tbh where she creates swords with them while putting fields over her enemies retinas and blinding them and then putting more fields over their synapses stopping their brain while having fields over herself and making herself invincible while having fields covering her team making them invincible and immune to telepaths and magic LMAO her being the only one that can do anything against Galactus is wildly comic accurate.  

Now the key here is that, given time, Reed Richards can think up something and build a machine to be OP as hell, which is why they are such an effective team. He’s directing them strategically while having a toned down version of the hulk as their tank, Johnny as a DPS that’s pumping out AOE damage with fire that doesn’t burn their own team, Sue as a hybrid defensive mage and offensive mage and Reed as the man with the plan strategist who himself is also fairly OP in a fight. He moves at just under lightspeed when he stretches around, and has stretched himself MILES AND MILES to shield a city from asteroids he can form mega fists, and has even twisted himself and grew his brain to think 4 dimensional and spin them into a new dimension on a moments notice. The FF are tough asf in the comics, but powers wise none of them come close to Sue.

replies.text:
The plan is Reed's. His intellect gave them all a fighting chance to begin with. While his superpower is of course the weakest, he stretches, he's not strong, he can't shoot lasers out of his eyes. His weapon is his mind and his heart, and both are on full display.
Johnny's interest in Silver Surfer makes him research her and consequently changes her heart so she saves his life. And BTW, Johnny was sacrificing his life for his baby nephew.
Ben did what was required of him to do, a great pilot and a superstrong being.
And Sue talked to people as a woman and a mother, and yes, this shit does impact people, at least normal people. She is also a great diplomat. And her powers have always been the strongest of them all, go blame comics for that. And she sacrifices herself to save her child.
And yes, they DID consider the giving the child up option. Reed and Sue had a whole scene about that. Or did you want to see humanity trying to rip a baby out of a superhero mother's arms to feed it to the monster from space?
If this is your definition of woke, then you've got bigger problems than Disney movies.
